location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday

secondary_archetype: Technical PM with people management / GPM

seniority.level: Staff / Group PM (likely IC7 or Director-equivalent)
level_signals: 7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers
Own multi-quarter vision and strategy for a core product area
Comp range $280K-$350K base, consistent with senior people-manager PM at Brex

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs.
7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs.
Have owned and scaled complex product areas in B2B, SaaS, or fintech, including driving measurable business outcomes across a multi-team portfolio.
Can set strategy across multiple product surfaces and translate it into roadmaps your team actually executes against.
Technically fluent enough to engage in architecture and systems tradeoffs with Engineering, not just defer to them.
Data-driven by default: you define the metrics, design the experiments, and use the results to make calls.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity, making prioritization tradeoffs with incomplete information, and bringing senior stakeholders along.
Clear, concise communicator — written and verbal — who can distill complex product problems into narratives that land with executives and cross-functional partners alike.

Experience incorporating AI/ML capabilities (e.g., LLMs, automation systems, predictive models) into customer-facing or internal products, with a strong understanding of where AI meaningfully improves user experience, efficiency, or decision quality.

Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to accelerate product discovery, analysis, experimentation, and execution (e.g., rapid prototyping, customer insight synthesis, requirement drafting, data exploration).

scope.surface: Core product area aligned to Brex's highest company priorities (spend management, AI-native automation, finance platform)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, and travel software
primary_metrics_inferred: Company-level business outcomes (not just feature shipping)
Measurable results tied to success metrics
core_responsibilities: Define and own the multi-quarter vision and strategy for a core product area aligned to Brex's highest company priorities
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers, providing coaching, feedback, and career development
Partner closely with Engineering, Design, Data, Risk, Legal, Compliance, Finance, and GTM leaders to deliver complex, cross-functional initiatives
Experience incorporating AI/ML capabilities into customer-facing or internal products
Leverage AI tools to accelerate product discovery, analysis, experimentation, and execution
Establish strong planning, prioritization, and execution frameworks across your portfolio

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($280K-$350K base)
Clear scope of responsibilities and ownership area defined
Company lists well-known customers (DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, SeatGeek)
Multiple office location options (SF, Seattle, Vancouver, NYC)
Explicit commitment to building a diverse team and inclusive culture
red_flags: Mandatory 3-days-per-week in-office (Mon/Wed/Thu) with only 4 weeks remote per year — limited flexibility for remote candidates
No explicit visa sponsorship information provided
AI/ML product experience listed under Responsibilities section rather than Requirements — may be treated as hard requirement despite structural placement creating ambiguity
No posting date available to assess freshness

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + people management scope + 7+ years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities language; not explicitly listed as KPIs)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on... candidate's location')
ambiguities: AI/ML product experience and AI tools usage items appear under 'Responsibilities' section rather than 'Requirements' section. They read as expectations/requirements in language ('Experience incorporating...', 'Demonstrated ability to...') but their structural placement is ambiguous. Treated as must_haves given unconditional phrasing.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
Vancouver is a Canadian city — the role may span US and Canada, but work authorization requirements are not differentiated by location.
